items.name: SNMP walk CPU temperature sensors
items.description:
MIB: MIKROTIK-MIB
Discovering CPU temperature sensors.

triggers.expression: last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/system.hw.firmware,#1)<>last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/system.hw.firmware,#2) and length(last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/system.hw.firmware))>0
triggers.name: MikroTik: Firmware has changed
triggers.description: Firmware version has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.expression: last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber,#1)<>last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber,#2) and length(last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber))>0
triggers.name: MikroTik: Device has been replaced
triggers.event_name: MikroTik: Device has been replaced (new serial number received)
triggers.description: Device serial number has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.expression: last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/system.sw.os[mtxrLicVersion.0],#1)<>last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/system.sw.os[mtxrLicVersion.0],#2) and length(last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/system.sw.os[mtxrLicVersion.0]))>0
triggers.name: MikroTik: Operating system description has changed
triggers.description: The description of the operating system has changed. Possible reasons are that the system has been updated or replaced. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

items.name: Total memory
items.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The size of the storage represented by this entry, in
units of hrStorageAllocationUnits. This object is
writable to allow remote configuration of the size of
the storage area in those cases where such an
operation makes sense and is possible on the
underlying system. For example, the amount of main
memory allocated to a buffer pool might be modified or
the amount of disk space allocated to virtual memory
might be modified.

items.name: Used memory
items.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of the storage represented by this entry that is allocated, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.

triggers.name: MikroTik: High memory utilization
triggers.description: The system is running out of free memory.

item_prototypes.name: #{#SNMPINDEX}: CPU utilization
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The average, over the last minute, of the percentage of time that this processor was not idle. Implementations may approximate this one minute smoothing period if necessary.

trigger_prototypes.name: MikroTik: #{#SNMPINDEX}: High CPU utilization
trigger_prototypes.description: CPU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

discovery_rules.name: Temperature CPU discovery
discovery_rules.description:
MIKROTIK-MIB::mtxrHlProcessorTemperature
Since temperature of CPU is not available on all Mikrotik hardware, this is done to avoid unsupported items.

item_prototypes.name: CPU: Temperature
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: MIKROTIK-MIB
mtxrHlProcessorTemperature Processor temperature in Celsius (degrees C).
Might be missing in entry models (RB750, RB450G..).

trigger_prototypes.name: MikroTik: CPU: Temperature is too low
trigger_prototypes.event_name: MikroTik: CPU: Temperature is too low: <{$TEMP_CRIT_LOW:"CPU"}

trigger_prototypes.name: MikroTik: CPU: Temperature {ITEM.LASTVALUE1} is above critical threshold
trigger_prototypes.event_name: MikroTik: CPU: Temperature {ITEM.LASTVALUE1} is above critical threshold: >{$TEMP_CRIT:"CPU"}
trigger_prototypes.description: This trigger uses temperature sensor values as well as temperature sensor status if available.

trigger_prototypes.name: MikroTik: CPU: Temperature {ITEM.LASTVALUE1} is above warning threshold
trigger_prototypes.event_name: MikroTik: CPU: Temperature {ITEM.LASTVALUE1} is above warning threshold: >{$TEMP_WARN:"CPU"}
trigger_prototypes.description: This trigger uses temperature sensor values as well as temperature sensor status if available.

dependencies.name: MikroTik: CPU: Temperature {ITEM.LASTVALUE1} is above critical threshold

discovery_rules.name: Temperature sensor discovery
discovery_rules.description:
MIKROTIK-MIB::mtxrHlTemperature
Since temperature sensor is not available on all Mikrotik hardware, this is done to avoid unsupported items.

item_prototypes.name: Device: Temperature
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: MIKROTIK-MIB
mtxrHlTemperature Device temperature in Celsius (degrees C).
Might be missing in entry models (RB750, RB450G..).

Reference: http://wiki.mikrotik.com/wiki/Manual:SNMP

trigger_prototypes.name: MikroTik: Device: Temperature is above critical threshold
trigger_prototypes.event_name: MikroTik: Device: Temperature is above critical threshold: >{$TEMP_CRIT:"Device"}
trigger_prototypes.description: This trigger uses temperature sensor values as well as temperature sensor status if available.

trigger_prototypes.name: MikroTik: Device: Temperature is above warning threshold
trigger_prototypes.event_name: MikroTik: Device: Temperature is above warning threshold: >{$TEMP_WARN:"Device"}
trigger_prototypes.description: This trigger uses temperature sensor values as well as temperature sensor status if available.

dependencies.name: MikroTik: Device: Temperature is above critical threshold

trigger_prototypes.name: MikroTik: Device: Temperature is too low
trigger_prototypes.event_name: MikroTik: Device: Temperature is too low: <{$TEMP_CRIT_LOW:"Device"}

item_prototypes.name: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Space utilization
item_prototypes.description: The space utilization expressed in % for Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}.

item_prototypes.name: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Total space
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The size of the storage represented by this entry, in
units of hrStorageAllocationUnits. This object is
writable to allow remote configuration of the size of
the storage area in those cases where such an
operation makes sense and is possible on the
underlying system. For example, the amount of main
memory allocated to a buffer pool might be modified or
the amount of disk space allocated to virtual memory
might be modified.

item_prototypes.name: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Used space
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of the storage represented by this entry that is allocated, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.

trigger_prototypes.expression:
last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/vfs.fs.pused[hrStorageSize.{#SNMPINDEX}])>{$VFS.FS.PUSED.MAX.CRIT:"Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}"} and
((last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/vfs.fs.total[hrStorageSize.{#SNMPINDEX}])-last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/vfs.fs.used[hrStorageSize.{#SNMPINDEX}]))<{$VFS.FS.FREE.MIN.CRIT:"Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}"} or timeleft(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/vfs.fs.pused[hrStorageSize.{#SNMPINDEX}],1h,100)<1d)
trigger_prototypes.name: MikroTik: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Disk space is critically low
trigger_prototypes.event_name: MikroTik: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Disk space is critically low (used > {$VFS.FS.PUSED.MAX.CRIT:"Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}"}%)
trigger_prototypes.description:
Two conditions should match: First, space utilization should be above {$VFS.FS.PUSED.MAX.CRIT:"Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}"}.
 Second condition should be one of the following:
 - The disk free space is less than {$VFS.FS.FREE.MIN.CRIT:"Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}"}.
 - The disk will be full in less than 24 hours.

trigger_prototypes.expression:
last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/vfs.fs.pused[hrStorageSize.{#SNMPINDEX}])>{$VFS.FS.PUSED.MAX.WARN:"Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}"} and
((last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/vfs.fs.total[hrStorageSize.{#SNMPINDEX}])-last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/vfs.fs.used[hrStorageSize.{#SNMPINDEX}]))<{$VFS.FS.FREE.MIN.WARN:"Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}"} or timeleft(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/vfs.fs.pused[hrStorageSize.{#SNMPINDEX}],1h,100)<1d)
trigger_prototypes.name: MikroTik: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Disk space is low
trigger_prototypes.event_name: MikroTik: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Disk space is low (used > {$VFS.FS.PUSED.MAX.WARN:"Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}"}%)
trigger_prototypes.description:
Two conditions should match: First, space utilization should be above {$VFS.FS.PUSED.MAX.WARN:"Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}"}.
 Second condition should be one of the following:
 - The disk free space is less than {$VFS.FS.FREE.MIN.WARN:"Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}"}.
 - The disk will be full in less than 24 hours.

dependencies.name: MikroTik: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Disk space is critically low
dependencies.expression:
last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/vfs.fs.pused[hrStorageSize.{#SNMPINDEX}])>{$VFS.FS.PUSED.MAX.CRIT:"Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}"} and
((last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/vfs.fs.total[hrStorageSize.{#SNMPINDEX}])-last(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/vfs.fs.used[hrStorageSize.{#SNMPINDEX}]))<{$VFS.FS.FREE.MIN.CRIT:"Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}"} or timeleft(/.MikroTik Generic by SNMP/vfs.fs.pused[hrStorageSize.{#SNMPINDEX}],1h,100)<1d)

macros.description: Warning threshold of error packet rate. Can be used with interface name as context.
Used as a threshold in the interface utilization trigger.
Link status trigger will be fired only for interfaces where the context macro equals "1".
Used in network interface discovery rule filters.

Used in network interface discovery rule filters.
Used for network interface discovery. Can be overridden on the host or linked template level.
Filters out `loopbacks`, `nulls`, docker `veth` links and `docker0 bridge` by default.
Used for network interface discovery. Can be overridden on the host or linked template level.
